# Workspace sync: create → add files → delete → sync detects stale

# Setup
exec git init
exec git config user.email test@test.com
exec git config user.name Test
exec $GOT_BIN init

# Create workspace and add files
exec $GOT_BIN workspace create sync-test
exec sh -c 'echo a > file_a.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo b > file_b.txt'
exec git add .
exec $GOT_BIN commit -m feat:files

exec $GOT_BIN workspace add-file sync-test file_a.txt
stdout 'Added file'

exec $GOT_BIN workspace add-file sync-test file_b.txt
stdout 'Added file'

# Verify 2 files tracked
exec $GOT_BIN workspace show sync-test
stdout 'file_a.txt'
stdout 'file_b.txt'

# Delete file_b.txt from disk
exec sh -c 'rm file_b.txt'

# Sync should detect stale file
exec $GOT_BIN workspace sync sync-test --no-cleanup
stdout '1 stale'
stdout 'file_b.txt'

# Now run cleanup
exec $GOT_BIN workspace sync sync-test
stdout 'Removed stale file'
